title: Falls-related injury review

examination.text: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.
examination.rationale: Supports clinician-led comprehensive falls assessment for falls-related injury review; no clinical decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk

investigations.text: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.
investigations.rationale: Supports entered non-complex fracture assessment context for falls-related injury review; no clinical decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

follow_up.text: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.
follow_up.rationale: Supports entered fall history and identification for falls assessment for falls-related injury review; no clinical decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — identifying people for falls assessment

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Fall mechanism, time, head impact, pain sites, weight-bearing, neurovascular findings, injuries, imaging and baseline falls risk are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for falls-related injury review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
